title: Another Comparison. Addressed To A Young Lady.
content:
Sweet stream that winds through yonder glade,
    Apt emblem of a virtuous maid�
    Silent and chaste she steals along,
    Far from the world�s gay busy throng;
    With gentle yet prevailing force,
    Intent upon her destined course;
    Graceful and useful all she does,
    Blessing and blest where�er she goes.
    Pure-bosom�d as that watery glass,
    And heaven reflected in her face.
meaning:
"Another Comparison. Addressed To A Young Lady." is a quintessential example of William Cowper's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
